text.title: Image Generation Tools Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failures in multi-provider image-generation services.

antipattern.symptom: Same prompt re-runs return different params silently; cache returns stale results.
antipattern.root-cause: Cache key includes prompt + provider only — not params (size, quality, seed).
antipattern.fix: Hash sha1(prompt + provider + canonical_json(params)). Recompute on any param drift (rule r2).

antipattern.symptom: Primary provider rate-limits; users see 5xx; no fallback fires.
antipattern.root-cause: No fallback chain wired; the abstraction passes errors through.
antipattern.fix: Implement fallback_chain with same inputs; cache populated by whichever provider succeeds (rule r4).

antipattern.symptom: Business code calls `OpenAI().images.generate(...)` directly; provider migration touches every caller.
antipattern.root-cause: No ImageService abstraction; provider SDK leaks into business code.
antipattern.fix: Force all callers through ImageService.generate(prompt, use_case, tenant_id) (rule r1).

antipattern.symptom: One tenant's runaway loop burns the org budget; nobody knows which tenant.
antipattern.root-cause: No per-tenant attribution emitted per call.
antipattern.fix: Emit {tenant_id, provider, cost_usd, use_case} per call to attribution sink (rule r5).

antipattern.symptom: Postmortem cannot link generated image to the input that produced it.
antipattern.root-cause: Audit log records storage_uri only — no input_hash or revised_prompt.
antipattern.fix: Persist {ts, tenant_id, provider_used, input_hash, revised_prompt, storage_uri, cost_usd, fallback_count} (rule r6).
